economic_notes: Nation holds commercial fishing quotas (crab, lobster, shrimp) granted by DFO at no monetary consideration, valued at $4,681,000. Fishing operations are a major economic activity. Wind energy project generates royalties. Forestry operations also conducted.
notes: Audit is qualified due to fishing quotas ($4,681,000) being carried as intangible assets on the balance sheet, which is not compliant with Canadian Public Sector Accounting Standards (PSAS require intangibles to be expensed). If adjusted, accumulated surplus and assets would each be reduced by $4,681,000. One subsidiary fully consolidated: Organisme de développement économique et communautaire Mic-Mac de Gaspé inc. (100%). A restatement was made to correct prior year transfer payment liability recognition, reducing 2019 opening accumulated surplus by $1,486,576. No schedule of remuneration was included in the extracted text. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($1,051,048) + Secrétariat aux affaires autochtones ($40,743) + Autres subventions ($911,946) = $2,003,737. OSR = total revenue minus total government transfers = $8,695,762 - $2,003,737 = $6,692,025. Fishing operations (Pêche sector) represent the largest expense at $3,884,287.
